name: OmopPractitionerRoleProviderView
title: PractitionerRole → provider (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
PractitionerRole enrichment of existing provider row. PractitionerRole does NOT create a new provider row; it adds specialty_concept_id, specialty_source_value, and care_site_id to the row created from the underlying Practitioner resource. One provider row per Practitioner; if multiple PractitionerRoles exist, choose the most recent or most specific.

Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: produce a flat row keeping source codes per vocabulary. Stage 2 joins vocab.concept to resolve concept_ids.

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR PractitionerRole.id; surrogate OMOP key derived (hash/sequence) downstream.

extension.valueString: surrogate key from hash/sequence/lookup of FHIR id

column.name: specialty
column.description: SNOMED or NUCC specialty mapped to OMOP concept. Represents the most common or most specific specialty. 0 if unmapped. fhir-to-omop-demo extracts from PractitionerRole.specialty[0].coding[0] (lines 10-23, 44).

extension.valueString: resolve CONCEPT reference; downstream maps to specialty_concept_id

column.name: specialty_text
column.description: Raw specialty code as it appears in source data. Includes physician specialties (internal medicine, emergency medicine) and allied health professionals. fhir-to-omop-demo uses specialty.concept_code (line 49).

column.name: managing_organization_id
column.description: Organization affiliation from PractitionerRole. This is the location that the provider primarily practices in. fhir-to-omop-demo uses location_ids[0] (line 45). omoponfhir creates a CareSite from the Practitioner's address if no PractitionerRole link exists.

extension.valueString: resolve CARE_SITE reference; downstream maps to care_site_id
